task.title: Execute the AgentCtx hardening backlog / step-01 Fix sync target semantics for context files
task.goal: Make context markdown syncing independent of pseudo-target names so using --targets does not silently skip .agentctx/context outputs. Add regression tests for the new sync behavior.
constraints: no placeholder public behavior without intent
every implementation step must pass the dual-agent evaluation gate before continuing
Only complete step step-01 before moving to the next step.
successCriteria: Each step passes the deterministic evaluator gate
The repo remains buildable and testable after every completed step
The highest-risk MVP gaps are reduced in priority order
Context files still sync when target filters are used
CLI package tests cover the regression

step.title: Fix sync target semantics for context files
step.instructions: Make context markdown syncing independent of pseudo-target names so using --targets does not silently skip .agentctx/context outputs. Add regression tests for the new sync behavior.
successCriteria: Context files still sync when target filters are used
CLI package tests cover the regression

decision.decision: Implement and evaluate step step-01: Fix sync target semantics for context files
decision.reason: Every meaningful change must pass the dual-agent gate before the task can advance.
alternativesConsidered: Implement multiple plan steps before running evaluation
Run one final evaluation only after the whole task is complete
risks: A passing deterministic gate does not guarantee the step is semantically complete.
Skipping the ordered plan would make the audit trail incomplete.

expectedEffects.performance: Runs deterministic checks on every gated step before continuing.
expectedEffects.tokenUsage: Encourages smaller, reviewable steps and less wasted iteration.
expectedEffects.usability: Creates a clear pass/revise/fail signal for each step.
expectedEffects.readability: Keeps implementation slices small and auditable.
expectedEffects.security: Blocks unsafe or broken states from being treated as complete.

evaluation.approvedNextStep: Proceed to step-02: Remove no-op public targets from the MVP surface
